batch

Class MediaRecord

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.MediaRecord

  • public final class MediaRecord
extends java.lang.Object
    Represents an immutable snapshot of a media file's properties read during a scan.

    This record encapsulates the physical location, raw metadata container, verified digital format, and chronological "truth" (Natural Date) of the file.

    This class is immutable provided that the supplied Metadata implementation is itself immutable.

    Since:
    1 May 2026
    Version:
    1.1
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field Description
      private java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime fileSystemDate  
      private boolean hasMetadataContainer  
      private java.nio.file.Path mediaFile  
      private DigitalSignature mediaFormat  
      private Metadata<?> metadata  

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      MediaRecord(java.nio.file.Path fpath, Metadata<?> meta, DigitalSignature sig, java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime ft)
      Constructs an immutable media record from the specified scan results.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      boolean equals(java.lang.Object other)
      Compares this record with another object.
      java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime getFileSystemDate()
      Returns the file system's last modified time recorded during the scan.
      DigitalSignature getMediaFormat()
      Returns the media format signature used to identify the file type, normally via magic numbers.
      Metadata<?> getMetadata()
      Returns the metadata container extracted from the media file.
      java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime getNaturalDate()
      Resolves the "chronological truth" for this file.
      java.nio.file.Path getPath()
      Returns the file system path to the media file.
      int hashCode()
      Computes a hash code based on the record's immutable state to ensure stable behaviour in hashed collections.
      boolean isHEIC()
      Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected HEIC standard.
      boolean isJPG()
      Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected JPG standard.
      boolean isMetadataEmpty()
      Indicates whether this media file lacks a valid embedded metadata container, such as EXIF or XMP.
      boolean isPNG()
      Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected PNG standard.
      boolean isTIF()
      Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected TIFF standard.
      boolean isVideoFormat()
      Returns whether this media file represents a recognised video format, such as MP4, MOV, or AVI.
      boolean isWebP()
      Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected WebP standard.
      java.lang.String toString()
      Returns a formatted string representation of the media record, suitable for CLI display or debug logging.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, finalize, getClass, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

      • mediaFile

        private final java.nio.file.Path mediaFile

      • metadata

        private final Metadata<?> metadata

      • mediaFormat

        private final DigitalSignature mediaFormat

      • fileSystemDate

        private final java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime fileSystemDate

      • hasMetadataContainer

        private final boolean hasMetadataContainer

    • Constructor Detail

      • MediaRecord

        public MediaRecord(java.nio.file.Path fpath,
                   Metadata<?> meta,
                   DigitalSignature sig,
                   java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime ft)
        Constructs an immutable media record from the specified scan results.
        Parameters:
        fpath - the path to the media file
        meta - the extracted metadata, or null if none exists
        sig - the detected media format signature
        ft - the file system timestamp recorded during scanning

    • Method Detail

      • getPath

        public java.nio.file.Path getPath()
        Returns the file system path to the media file.
        Returns:
        the media file path

      • getMetadata

        public Metadata<?> getMetadata()
        Returns the metadata container extracted from the media file.
        Returns:
        the metadata instance, or null if no metadata was found

      • getMediaFormat

        public DigitalSignature getMediaFormat()
        Returns the media format signature used to identify the file type, normally via magic numbers.
        Returns:
        the media format type

      • isMetadataEmpty

        public boolean isMetadataEmpty()
        Indicates whether this media file lacks a valid embedded metadata container, such as EXIF or XMP.
        Returns:
        true if the file lacks metadata, otherwise false

      • isJPG

        public boolean isJPG()
        Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected JPG standard.
        Returns:
        true if JPG, otherwise false

      • isPNG

        public boolean isPNG()
        Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected PNG standard.
        Returns:
        true if PNG, otherwise false

      • isTIF

        public boolean isTIF()
        Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected TIFF standard.
        Returns:
        true if TIFF, otherwise false

      • isHEIC

        public boolean isHEIC()
        Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected HEIC standard.
        Returns:
        true if HEIC, otherwise false

      • isWebP

        public boolean isWebP()
        Checks if the media file's digital signature matches the expected WebP standard.
        Returns:
        true if WebP, otherwise false

      • isVideoFormat

        public boolean isVideoFormat()
        Returns whether this media file represents a recognised video format, such as MP4, MOV, or AVI.
        Returns:
        true if the media is a video, otherwise false

      • getFileSystemDate

        public java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime getFileSystemDate()
        Returns the file system's last modified time recorded during the scan.
        Returns:
        the file system date

      • getNaturalDate

        public java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime getNaturalDate()
        Resolves the "chronological truth" for this file. Prioritises embedded metadata timestamps before falling back to the file system's last modified time.
        Returns:
        the most accurate timestamp available for this media

      • equals

        public boolean equals(java.lang.Object other)
        Compares this record with another object. Two records are considered equal if they point to the same path and share the same metadata and format state.
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
        Parameters:
        other - the object to compare
        Returns:
        true if the objects are equal, otherwise false

      • hashCode

        public int hashCode()
        Computes a hash code based on the record's immutable state to ensure stable behaviour in hashed collections.
        Overrides:
        hashCode in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        the hash code for this object

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Returns a formatted string representation of the media record, suitable for CLI display or debug logging.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a multi-line formatted string containing file details
